item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow, i donno why i loved this as much as i did, but i really love this

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
it says "remaining three photographs" still, but there's only one due to the change. perhaps that could be fixed?

edit: thanks!

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The first image can be traced back to here, which states that the image was taken by NASA, whose images fall into public domain and thus are compliant with CC 3.0.

The second image is from here, which falls in the public domain and thus is compliant with CC 3.0.

The third and fourth images could not be reliably sourced and are assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, the last two images have been removed.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so in 2013, someone had just learned the second meaning of the word 'precipitate', huh? pretty cool. i like it.

item.title: Attempting to explain this SCP
item.content:encoded: Alright. I going to try and make as much sense of this as I can. So, this alien race either created, or has been watching earth for a while. They are entertained by us, and are making a joke out of putting test subjects into horrifyingly painful states, contorting their bodies into a disgusting mess. I'm probably wrong, I did enjoy this SCP though, you get an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, shit. This is scarily accurate. I'm kind of embarrassed by this SCP nowadays, and so couldn't bring myself to view this discussion page until now. I think I want to go back and edit it in accordance with some of the issues raised here. But anyway, if this pic is a depiction of 2222, then I'm flattered.

Reverse image search reveals that it's a cover to a comic called 30 Days of Night: https://sayhellospaceman.blogspot.co.uk/2015/02/30-days-of-night-dead-space-2006.html?m=1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The photos really killed it for me. It just seems like they have no use. Plus, they're just images of random things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good, though I have a couple issues with this.

1. Why send the bodies into space, and risk NASA or any other space agencies potentially spotting them? It would make more sense to euthanize them and store them in a morgue area aboard the ship.

2. I know everyone else has already said this, but why involve an O5? They're like the top of the top brass, inconsistent canon or not there's no way they'd be doing field work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I personally would like to know whether

embedded within ████ (█████ ████████ █████ █████████████)

means China National Space Administration. That's the closest match letter-wise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello. Russian Foundation branch member here, looking for explaination of the following passage:

The following events occurred between the dates of ██/██/████ and ██/██/████ following successful interior surveillance within SCP-2222 after handheld digital cameras within the chamber exhibited non-malfunction. The preceding information thus warranted inclusion and exploration within SCP-2222, conducted by seven (7) D-Class personnel resituated upon Orbital Unit-09 with preliminary sedation administrated beforehand, alongside five (5) Level 4-5 Foundation operatives with the responsibility of Unit maintenance and safety, alongside communication with D-Class personnel. Current Unit personnel were granted temporary leave, with long-term signal failure occurring across all ████ so as to maintain the undetectability of the response force relocating the personnel. It is to be of note that D-Class personnel were unaware of SCP-2222's anomalous effects prior to their exposure to SCP-2222.

If i am getting it right, it is said here that the common Orbital Unit-09 outfit was replaced with some high-level staff to overlook the research conducted by disposable personnel. Now, the part i need help with is the one about "preceding information". Where is this information actually given - is it just right in the previous sentence about cameras or elsewhere? The other confusing place is that with the "long-term signal failure occurring across all ████". Was that signal falure an intentional action of the Foundation staff commited as a concealment measure, or did it have a different origin? Although the original author`s feedback would be the most definitive kind of an answer, hints from other native-english members are also appreciated. Thank you in advance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Found a picture that seems very relevant to this particular Scip. Got it from /tg/ some while ago and not able to find a source.

+ There's something in the airlock
- kcloria eht ni gnihtemos s'erehT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sweet, so it's Webway portal to Comorragh. I'm sure Asdrubael Vect is amused by this funny, primitive Holy Terra expies.

item.title: Good one
item.content:encoded:
This gets an upvote from me.

The only thing that bothered me was the line 'Oh, God- Uh, Okay, crew. Get him up. Take him to the lab for a final run-down before shooting him off.; The offhand manner in which the doctor sends a former level 4 operative to his death is rather strange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Classification is based on difficulty to contain, not danger or malicious intent. If no one enters the airlock, nothing happens, so it Would'nt be a keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i actually did feel light headed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not feeling it. interested in the concept, but the torture porn and wasteful deployment of d-class bores me. neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for effort
+1 for the idea
+1 for the execution
+1 for making an O5 work on the field (deal with it)
+1 for story

Lovely, congratz!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew I wasn't the only one :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this amused me upon first arrival to this article:

http://i.imgur.com/GPUbmTQ.png

…Sadly, my +1 has brought it to 112, so the moment has passed. C'est la vie or whatnot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am not disregarding this kind of effort. That was one hell of a story.

.1+ Etovpu

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a portal to the Dark Eldar? A few nags here and there, but since I kept thinking "this would be a pretty decent sci-fi movie" upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely adore this skip. It's one of the first SCPs I read, and part of the reason why I aspire to contribute more to this site.

Between the explorations and the audio log, it gave me a chilling sense of immersion (which is something that I always enjoy when reading something). Others would say its a bit lengthy, but I believe it pays off well in the end.

If I had the power to, I would upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt that this skip had an interesting concept. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually have an issue with the extra part. If the speech was reversed back from being backwards, then the agent would be saying lairtessetartxe

Unless you're counting it as a compound word

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oooooooohhhhh……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be classified as keter? It seems to have malicious intent based on the writings on the first agent everything, not to mention the rather painful sounding effect it has on any careless individualsone it doesn't like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is way too much useless material in this article. It's like reading a version of the Lorax where Dr. Seuss spends twelve pages talking about how brown the trees are before the Lorax even shows up. It's really exhausting to read the whole thing. If the whole point was to show that the Foundation was withholding information than just do it once, don't do it all the time.

Also paragraphs exist. The whole article is populated by thick blocks of text that are really cumbersome to scroll through. I can't even tell you what the SCP did at this point, I think it was mentioned but all I can recall is endless menial details. Did it eat people or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This might just be my new favourite SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It reminds me of the Apollo 18 movie, does it remind you of it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this until the last log, the wording and the reveal kind of killed it for me :/. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can view the page source by clicking the "+ Options" link at the bottom of the page and clicking "Page Source".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the interesting concept and the (horror?) of the article. Very interesting and enjoyable to read. The warnings are a nice touch as well. How did you incorporate them in are they images or a special sort of coding?

Anyways +1 congratulations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because everyone who needs to know that information is already involved in it. It's not describing a directive that personnel would need to know in order to follow, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I love the rest, but why is there censorship in the containment procedures? I understood that was a massive no-no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ya, Loyal. It reminded me of that too…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot! I'm a bit curious how the final test log was hijacked by an alien transmission (in English, no less), but it adds quite a bit to the flavor of the piece, so it's no big deal. My understanding of what was going on was turned on its head at least twice while reading this piece, so kudos for good plot twists!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminded me of the Goosebumps book, "One Day at Horrorland".
Anyone else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So that's how Cenobites like to amuse themselves in between puzzle box openings. Good to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's gone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool. Also, I'd like you to see my new SCP, SCP-1632. Here's the link.
http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/scp-1632

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spot on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's cool. So basically, SCP-2222 is a vault owned by aliens who are desperate for cruel entertainment, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this article very interesting. +1. But, when ████ ██████████ said "extra-" and passed out, was that implying that he saw extraterrestrials?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agent Blackbox's voice is the best voice ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welp, I sure am happy that due to work I couldnt sleep anyway for the next 12 hours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I ended up no-voting this one. Stylistically this reminds me a lot of Event Horizon, which I like, and the concept of being trapped in space aboard a cramped satellite with a horrific thing that would love nothing more than to turn you inside-out is solidly horrifying.

It goes a little far, though, with the goofy backmasking and the messages written on peoples' skin. And as it keeps going, the piece loses the mood that made me like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lots of weaknesses, lots of strengths, but one great bit that I noticed:

Individual vocalisations from sentient organisms such as Rattus rattus also exhibit this impairment.

Now I know that rats meet a few reasonable definitions for "sentience," and would be amused if the Foundation had chosen one of those more open definitions to be "correct." Yet SCP-1318 suggests otherwise, in which case the (in-setting) author of the article is significantly (and very subtly) under the SCP-1318 effect!

Edit: oops- Just realized that SCP-1318 is rattus norwegicus, not rattus rattus… hmmm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked some of it, but…

+ Let's see whats behind airlock number one!
See, it's funny because it's like a tv show and ok I'll stop now

Like a lot of the others, I have to commend you on the execution for the most part, but the concept just doesn't work well for me. Part of it is, I think, that the end note (And a couple of other places, in truth) hits you over the head with the concept. It leaves very little room for other interpretations, which bothers me because the concept of an alien reality show leaves me a bit cold.

I mean, the Foundation could have figured that out to at least some degree by some of the earlier explanations (what with the humans being entertaining thing). Why not just sit back and watch the door at that point? Why feed the thing, even with D-class? Why give a monster what it wants when we aren't sure it is required for containment, and we know for certain that giving it what it wants makes it want more?

And finally, why is there an O-5 here? I know there is no cannon, but when I first saw it I was actually expecting some sort of twist or something, that this thing has some sort of memetic qualities that resulted in it somehow grabbing an 05, who went rogue used his authority to get himself up there before the rest of the 05s could stop him. Or maybe it's not memetic at all, and he is just mad and obsessed, which puts the whole throwing D-class into the meat grinder into a whole new light. Instead, he's just… there.

Which leads me to the whole bludgeoning us with your concept point. I could explain the 05s presence with my headcannon, building a story up about that instead of an alien tv show. But I can't do that to some degree, because you've made your own interpretation so clear (notably with the last entry) that I feel like I am advocating for an alternative explanation than what was intended instead of filling in the blanks, which turns me off of the whole thing.

Anyway, I'll come back to it and take another look in a bit and consider a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. Good touch with the rats and thanks for the reply and explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was actually going for the reality show hypothesis, but that is actually a much better way of interpreting this.

As for the rats, they're for analysis purposes; the researchers onboard release rats within the airlock so as to observe the new physical/vocal characteristics caused by SCP-2000. Not a significant area to note, but I wanted to subtly highlight the comparable similarity between D-Class personnel and everyday animal test subjects, such as rats. Also, extensive testing with D-Class personnel would quickly drain Foundation resources (as highlighted within one of the guides somewhere which I can't find), so test rats are generally a better option.

[+] KIA'd personnel - [SPOILERS]
[-]

Yeah, so Exploration VI was supposed to imply that the entities within the airlock actually have control over the entire space station, not just SCP-2000, and that this was a rare case in which they opened the inner door killed the personnel onboard the rest of the station. The reason why they don't take advantage of this control more often is due to the fact that Meschk-Vordhosbn is the comedic centre of the show for the aliens, but Exploration VI yielded an exclusive broadcast in which the rest of the space station was exposed to the general extraterrestrial public. Kind of like a celebratory christmas special in which extra blood is shed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okey, okey, okey,…

Why were the three L4,L4, and L5 KIAd?

Also, is this basically a <Headcanon> reality show for aliens? </headcanon>

I sort of didn't get why do they allow rats in. Can you drop me a clue, please?

+1

_

EDIT: Not a reality show, but more of a Hell's Kitchen. As in, who can "prepare" their human better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I licked the concept of having two lvl5 lvls (lvl5 & LvlO5)
but O5 in research (and as far as I have understood In space on an anomalous Space vessel) makes me downvote it.

There is a good reason for it. And the one D-Class knowing personal information about the present O5 shows exactly why that rule is in place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, solid points you've brought up there - It's likely that I'll make use of the major editing period, so watch the article for changes. Thanks for your input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
alien porn? D-90123 really into masochism?

Mmmmmmmmmmrrrrrrrrrrbrbrbbrbrmrmbmbrmbmbrmsbnnzbmmzmnbbmmm…

I really want to love it, and the execution is spectacular, but somewhere towards the end, the mystery seems to die out, and it feels more like "just" an alien reality TV show, with porny overtones. I feel like this would be immensely horrifying if it essentially ended with Experiment III, and/or did not have the aliens appear or be audible on tape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-82111: Yeah.

first sentence of d-class doesnt scream "im badass but what is this?"

+1.
will revisit after more plot.

edit: more plot has been seen. Still +1.
smile, youre on tv.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Link removed, D-Class designations fixed, and comprise/composed inclusions revised.

Thanks for your input on this, I wouldn't've caught those otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You use D-90123 is some places and D-90124 in others. Your "composes"/"comprises" are all backwards. In the last collapsible, there's a link to a non-existent page; I don't think this was intentional.

I like some bits of this, like the very incomprehensible aliens and the second part of the last note. However, there is too much expungement, too much pointless sections that don't add much if anything, and no real closure. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You addressed a number of issues I had, and successfully, I think. However… it still is faceplanting for me on concept. I'm not particularly inclined to be scared or interested by overdramatic aliens who get off way too much on turning humans inside out, and this article doesn't make them work for me.
